pi_items.source: Police radio codes documentation. 10-31 is the official police code for 'Crime in Progress' (APCO standard established 1940). While the task describes an armed robbery specifically, the call type is correctly set to the general crime-in-progress code.
GTA Wiki (https://gta.fandom.com/wiki/Vinewood_Boulevard_(HD_Universe)). Confirmed as real location in Grand Theft Auto V running east-west through Downtown Vinewood, Los Santos.
GTA Wiki (https://gta.fandom.com/wiki/Hawick_Avenue). Confirmed as real location in Grand Theft Auto V running through Burton and Hawick neighborhoods in Los Santos.

GTA V license plate format verification. License plates in GTA V follow flexible formats with up to 8 characters. RPZ-7851 (8 chars with hyphen) is within valid format range. Custom plates are supported in GTA V via iFruit app and License Plate Creator.
README.md database schema (line 49): 'Trevor Philips: id=3 (existing seed data — already has 2 warrants for other offenses)'. Confirmed via export_result.sh warrant verification logic (line 106).
GTA Wiki (https://gta.fandom.com/wiki/Trevor_Philips). Confirmed as one of three playable protagonists in Grand Theft Auto V. Database confirmed in README.md as ncic_names id=3.

U.S. job occupation verified via multiple sources. Senior Police Dispatchers are real law enforcement positions responsible for receiving emergency calls, prioritizing dispatch, and managing CAD systems. Standard role in emergency response coordination.

privileged_info_summary: The armed_robbery_response task is a synthetic scenario simulation set in Grand Theft Auto V's fictional Los Santos. All metadata claims are verified as accurate: the 10-31 police code is the authentic APCO standard for Crime in Progress; Vinewood Boulevard and Hawick Avenue are confirmed real locations in GTA V; the Karin Kuruma and license plate format RPZ-7851 are authentic to GTA V; Trevor Philips is a real protagonist character with database ID 3; and Senior Police Dispatcher is a verified law enforcement profession. The scenario does not correspond to any canonical GTA V mission but uses authentic game elements and real law enforcement terminology integrated into an OpenCAD-based roleplay dispatch simulation.
